title: [ConstantFolding] Fix bitcasting vectors with non-integer ratios
author: varev-dev
description:
This patch fixes a crash when constant folding bitcast from a vector with larger elements to a vector with smaller ones, where the element count ratio is not an integer (e.g., <3 x i32> to <4 x i24>). 

Also this patch fixes existing miscompilation when bitcasting a vector with smaller elements into vector with larger ones, where the element count ratio is not an integer (e.g., <4 x i24> to <3 x i32>). 

Previously the logic assumed a uniform ratio, leading to 
- an incorrect number of result elements and subsequent crashes in RAUW, or
- miscompilation, bytes lost in process.

Fixes #179626 
Fixes #181195

comments.author: varev-dev
comments.body:
Update regarding the fix approach: Following the feedback and further debugging I confirmed that the root cause lies in `lib/Analysis/ConstantFolding.cpp`, The function FoldBitCast incorrectly calculates the element ratio for vector types that don't divide evenly (e.g., casting 3 x i32 to 4 x i24), leading to a result with the wrong number of elements.

I am rewriting this patch to fix `ConstantFolding.cpp` directly. I will implement a check to handle these non-uniform vector bitcasts correctly.

comments.author: varev-dev
varev-dev
comments.body:
Gentle ping. 

I have implemented the previous review suggestions: recuded the test and replaced the workaround with proper fix in `ConstantFolding::FoldBitCast` for cases where `SrcBitSize % DstBitSize != 0`.

I would appreciate another review of the current implementation. Thank you

comments.author: varev-dev
comments.body: @nikic I have addressed all requested changes in the latest commits. Since the PR is still marked as 'Changes Required', it might be filtered out of the review queue. Could you please take a look and dismiss the block if the current state looks good?

comments.author: cardigan1008
comments.body:
Hi, there is a related miscompilation test case:

```llvm
define <2 x i60> @rev_nonint_ratio() {
  %c = bitcast <5 x i24> <i24 1, i24 2, i24 3, i24 4, i24 5> to <2 x i60>
  ret <2 x i60> %c
}
```

Alive2 proof: https://alive2.llvm.org/ce/z/RTtbvU

> Note: This is a review assisted with a self-built AI agent. The reproducer was validated manually. Please let me know if anything is wrong.

The patch fixed non-integer-ratio handling only for `NumDstElt > NumSrcElt` (widening to smaller elements) by adding bit-stream logic. It did not symmetrically fix the reverse compression path (`NumDstElt < NumSrcElt`), leaving old floor-ratio packing logic.

The `NumDstElt < NumSrcElt` path still uses `Ratio = NumSrcElt / NumDstElt` integer truncation and assumes integral grouping, which is invalid for non-integer element-count ratios (here 5/2).

comments.author: varev-dev
comments.body: @cardigan1008 Good catch, the case you described was also reported in #181195. I am working on this path right now and will update this PR shortly to cover the complete non-byte-sized vector cast logic.
